content.question: What are the magnitude and mechanisms of demand-side resistance to track elimination among middle-class parents? Would track abolition coupled with alternative quality-signaling mechanisms (school-based reputation, specialized curricula, magnet-school competition) reduce this resistance?
content.reduction_path: Conduct representative survey of parent preferences on track continuation versus integration, with price-trade-off and alternative quality-signal scenarios; ethnographic study of middle-class school choice in systems that recently eliminated tracking; pilot small-scale 'inclusive-but-differentiated' school models to test parent and student acceptance
